items.correct: Development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs — balancing economic, social and environmental goals.
items.misconception: Sustainable development means stopping all economic growth.
items.random_wrong: Sustainable development means using only solar power.
items.why: Students treat sustainability as anti-development. The Brundtland definition is about balance and intergenerational equity — not zero growth or one specific technology.

items.correct: Snow accumulates in a north-east-facing hollow, compacts to ice, and rotational movement combined with plucking and abrasion deepens the hollow into an armchair shape; meltwater can fill it as a tarn.
items.misconception: A corrie is just a small lake at the top of a mountain.
items.random_wrong: A corrie forms when a river dries up high in the mountains.
items.why: The corrie is the eroded hollow; the tarn (lake) is just the post-glacial water that fills it. Aspect matters — north-east in the Northern Hemisphere because of shade and prevailing wind.

items.q: Why do some rivers form deltas and others form estuaries?
items.correct: Deltas form where rivers deposit sediment faster than the sea can remove it (calm seas, heavy load); estuaries form where tidal energy removes sediment (strong tides).
items.misconception: Deltas form on big rivers and estuaries on small ones.
items.random_wrong: Deltas form in cold climates and estuaries in warm climates.
items.why: Size doesn't determine the form. The balance of sediment supply vs marine energy does — the Nile (delta) and Thames (estuary) are similar size but in different sea conditions.

items.q: What is the replacement fertility rate?
items.why: It's 2.1. “1.5” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Boserup. “Malthus” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: World average ecological footprint (gha per capita)?
items.why: The real figure is 3 gha. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
